title: aglaia elaeagnoidea var. beddomei
intro: Aglaia elaeagnoidea var. beddomei is a rare and endangered species of plant, belonging to the Meliaceae family, which is native to the Western Ghats in India. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Aglaia elaeagnoidea var. beddomei typically involve extracting its bioactive compounds through decoctions, infusions, or oil extractions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
